description: British Columbia introduced Canada's first revenue-neutral carbon tax in 2008, facing initial intense political backlash that subsided as dividends were issued. The policy was eventually accepted by both major parties by 2013.

key_events.description: Gordon Campbell government introduces the tax under intense criticism.
First round of personal tax cuts tied to the carbon tax implementation.
Public polling shifts as citizens see dividend rebates on income taxes.
NDP drops 'axe the tax' campaign slogan in provincial election.

calibration_notes: Useful for understanding opinion shifts when direct financial rebates are provided to voters.
